definition: The current configuration of elevated rates vol (MOVE ~100-120), suppressed equity vol (VIX ~13-18), and low G10 FX vol (~7-9%) is historically anomalous, structurally driven by four persistent factors (BoJ normalization uncertainty, Fed terminal rate ambiguity, $2T+ annual Treasury issuance, basis trade fragility at $800B-$1T), and historically unstable with no precedent for persisting as a steady state.
evidence: Six of eight agents independently identified the divergent configuration
Three historical analogues (2006-07, 2014, 2017-18) all preceded transitions within 6-18 months
MOVE above 95 for 18+ months — longest sustained elevation since 2008-2012
No historical instance found where this configuration persisted as steady state
Convergence from deductive, inductive, NK, Post-Keynesian, and rates-specialist frameworks
open_questions: Is this a permanent structural feature or transitional state?
Does the Kalecki fiscal channel sustain the divergence indefinitely?
Would a formal structural break test on the MOVE-VIX relationship show a regime change?
What is the correct structural floor for MOVE in the current environment — 80, 100, or 120+?
